title: EvidenceCopula: Tail Dependence Between Trust and Statistical Significance in Cochrane Meta-Analyses
summary: Copula-based analysis of dependence between trust scores and statistical significance (|z|) across 403 Cochrane meta-analyses reveals symmetric inverse dependence best captured by the Frank copula.
data: 403 Cochrane systematic reviews from EvidenceOracle feature matrix; trust score = 0.6 x robustness + 0.4 x quality; p-values from Egger's test or derived from fraction significant
body: Is the joint distribution of methodological trust and statistical significance in Cochrane meta-analyses captured by copulas with tail concentration or symmetric structure? We fitted Clayton, Frank, and Gumbel copulas to pseudo-observations of trust score and absolute z-statistic from 403 Cochrane systematic reviews, selecting by AIC. The Frank copula provided the best fit (theta = -2.676, AIC = -70.2) versus Clayton (AIC = 6.4) and Gumbel (AIC = 9.5). Kendall tau was -0.279 (95% CI: -0.357 to -0.194, p < 0.001) and Spearman rho was -0.412 (p < 0.001). Findings were consistent across the four domains with at least ten reviews each. High-trust reviews are modestly but reliably less likely to report inflated significance, with no tail concentration (lambda_L = lambda_U = 0). These results apply to Cochrane reviews with available p-values and may not extend to grey literature.

sentences.text: Is the joint distribution of methodological trust and statistical significance in Cochrane meta-analyses captured by copulas with tail concentration or symmetric structure?
We fitted Clayton, Frank, and Gumbel copulas to pseudo-observations of trust score and absolute z-statistic from 403 Cochrane systematic reviews, selecting by AIC.
The Frank copula provided the best fit (theta = -2.676, AIC = -70.2) versus Clayton (AIC = 6.4) and Gumbel (AIC = 9.5).
Kendall tau was -0.279 (95% CI: -0.357 to -0.194, p < 0.001) and Spearman rho was -0.412 (p < 0.001).
Findings were consistent across the four domains with at least ten reviews each.
High-trust reviews are modestly but reliably less likely to report inflated significance, with no tail concentration (lambda_L = lambda_U = 0).
These results apply to Cochrane reviews with available p-values and may not extend to grey literature.

primary_plot.caption: Pseudo-observations (U, V) = (rank trust / n+1, rank |z| / n+1) for 403 Cochrane meta-analyses. Negative slope confirms Frank copula inverse dependence.
